title: abutilon bastardioides
intro: Abutilon bastardioides, commonly known as the Bastard mallow or African mallow, is a flowering plant species in the mallow family, Malvaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where its roots and leaves are used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Abutilon bastardioides include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Abutilon bastardioides can be made by infusing its roots and leaves in water or by mixing its dried and powdered form with other herbal ingredients to create teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
